chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male patient presented with syncope followed by epigastric pain.
history: The patient had a history of hypertension and hyperlipidemia. He was admitted for syncope followed by epigastric pain. Increased hs-TnI peak values and more pronounced ST-segment elevation in V3 than other leads were noted.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 80 bpm. Physical examination revealed no significant abnormalities.
labs: ECG: ST-segment elevation in V3, ST depression in V2 and V4, negative T waves in many leads. Bloods: hs-TnI peak values elevated.
correct_diagnosis: Acute coronary syndrome with multivessel coronary artery disease
differentials: Acute coronary syndrome with multivessel coronary artery disease
Myocardial infarction with ST-elevation in a single lead
correct_ranking: Acute coronary syndrome with multivessel coronary artery disease
Myocardial infarction with ST-elevation in a single lead
